subject: ⚠️ URGENT: Your account has been limited
body:
Dear Valued Customer,

We have detected unusual activity on your PayPal account. To avoid permanent suspension, you must verify your information within 24 hours.

Click the link below to restore your account access immediately:

https://paypal-secure-verify.com/restore?user=confirm

Failure to respond will result in permanent account closure.

PayPal Security Department
explanation: This is a phishing email using urgency tactics and a spoofed sender domain ('paypa1' with a number 1 instead of letter l).
red_flags: Urgent language designed to create panic
Generic greeting 'Valued Customer' instead of your name

subject: Your pull request was merged
body:
Hi there,

Your pull request #42 'Fix authentication bug' has been merged into main by octocat.

View the pull request: https://github.com/your-repo/pull/42

You're receiving this because you're subscribed to this repository. Unsubscribe from these emails in your notification settings.

GitHub, Inc.
explanation: Legitimate GitHub notification. Sender is from the official github.com domain, link points to github.com, and content matches expected PR notifications.
trust_indicators: Contains specific PR details (number, title, merger)

subject: Password expires TODAY - Reset required
body:
Hello Employee,

Your corporate password will expire in 2 hours. All employees must reset their passwords through our new secure portal immediately.

Reset Password Now: http://reset.company-support-portal.net/reset

If you do not reset your password, you will be locked out of all company systems including email, VPN, and internal tools.

IT Department
explanation: Classic spear-phishing targeting corporate employees. The domain is not a company domain, uses HTTP instead of HTTPS, and creates artificial urgency.
red_flags: Domain 'company-support-portal.net' is generic, not your company's domain
Uses HTTP instead of HTTPS — no encryption
Extreme urgency: '2 hours' to create panic
Threatens lockout from all systems simultaneously

subject: Your receipt from Spotify
body:
Hi Sadaf,

Thanks for your payment. Here's your receipt:

Premium Individual Plan - Monthly
Amount: $9.99
Date: November 14, 2024
Order #SP-2847361

Manage your subscription at spotify.com/account

Questions? Visit spotify.com/help

© 2024 Spotify AB
explanation: Legitimate Spotify receipt email. Uses official domain, addresses user by name, contains specific transaction details, and links only go to spotify.com.
trust_indicators: Specific order number and transaction details
No requests for action or credentials

subject: Sign-in attempt from Russia - Action Required
body:
Microsoft Account Alert

Unusual sign-in activity detected!

Location: Moscow, Russia
Time: 3:42 AM
Device: Unknown Windows PC

If this wasn't you, immediately secure your account:
→ Click here to secure account

Ignoring this alert may result in complete account compromise and data loss.

Microsoft Security
explanation: Geo-scare phishing. The misspelled domain 'microsofft-security.com' gives it away. These emails exploit fear of unauthorized access to steal credentials.
red_flags: Vague 'Click here' link without showing destination

subject: Anna Schmidt viewed your profile
body:
Hi Sadaf,

Anna Schmidt, Senior Recruiter at TechCorp Vienna, viewed your profile.

See who's viewed your profile: linkedin.com/in/you/who-viewed

You appear in 12 searches this week.

LinkedIn Corporation, 1000 West Maude Avenue, Sunnyvale, CA 94085
explanation: Legitimate LinkedIn notification. Official domain, contains specific viewer details, standard physical address in footer, and no suspicious requests.
trust_indicators: Specific person name and company mentioned

subject: Your order #114-8273645 could not be delivered
body:
Dear Amazon Customer,

We attempted to deliver your package but were unable to complete delivery due to an incorrect address.

Your package will be returned to sender in 48 hours unless you update your delivery details.

Update Delivery Address: http://amazon-deliveries-tracking.info/update

Order total: $247.99
Item: [Package Contents]

Amazon Logistics
explanation: Delivery-themed phishing. Amazon uses amazon.com, never third-party domains. The vague 'Package Contents' and generic format confirm this is fake.
red_flags: Vague 'Package Contents' — real orders show item names
Link goes to suspicious third-party domain

subject: New sign-in to your Google Account
body:
Hi Sadaf,

Your Google Account was just signed in to from:

Device: Chrome on Windows
Location: Budapest, Hungary
Time: Today, 2:15 PM

If this was you, you don't need to do anything.

If you didn't sign in, check your account activity:
myaccount.google.com/security

The Google Accounts Team
explanation: Legitimate Google sign-in alert. Uses official accounts.google.com sender, provides non-urgent guidance, link goes to myaccount.google.com, and matches your actual location.
trust_indicators: No panic — says 'you don't need to do anything' if it was you
